message:
docs(quickstart): Add solo-user workflow guidance

Solo users (both agent and operator) now have clear guidance on how to
trigger learning manually without scheduled reflection:
- Manual reflection after work or weekly
- Agent-native onboarding workflow

Closes: cass_memory_system-uom9

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

1. success-diary.yaml - Successful session with accomplishments
2. failure-diary.yaml - Failed session with challenges
3. mixed-diary.yaml - Session with mixed outcomes
4. rich-diary.yaml - All fields populated (relatedSessions, searchAnchors, etc.)
5. minimal-diary.yaml - Only required fields
6. multi-agent-diary.yaml - Related sessions from different agents

Each should:
- Be valid against DiaryEntrySchema
- Have realistic timestamps
- Include realistic session paths
- Cover the 5 structured content arrays (accomplishments, decisions, challenges, preferences, keyLearnings)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:22:16.201911-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:13:03.897506-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:43.941401-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b96","title":"[Generator] generateSuggestedQueries - Follow-up search suggestions","description":"Generate human-readable cass search suggestions for follow-up investigation.

SIGNATURE:
function generateSuggestedQueries(task: string, keywords: string[]): string[]

PURPOSE:
Provide ready-to-run cass commands that the user can execute to dig deeper if the provided context isn't sufficient. Helps with iterative investigation.

OUTPUT:
Array of 3-5 formatted cass command strings

FORMAT:
'cass search "{query}" --days {days} [--agent {agent}]'

EXAMPLE:
Task: 'Fix authentication timeout bug'
Keywords: ['authentication', 'timeout', 'token', 'session', 'jwt']

Output: [
  'cass search "authentication timeout" --days 30',
  'cass search "token refresh" --days 60',
  'cass search "session expiry" --agent claude',
  'cass search "jwt validation error" --days 90'
]

QUERY GENERATION STRATEGY:
1. Use top 2-3 keyword combinations from keywords array
2. Add task-derived queries (extract noun phrases)
3. Vary --days parameter (30, 60, 90) for different time scopes
4. Include --agent filter if config.preferredAgent is set
5. Prioritize error/problem-oriented queries ('error', 'fix', 'bug')

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Escape quotes in query strings
- Limit to 5 suggestions (avoid overwhelming)
- Include at least one broad query (first keyword only)
- Include at least one specific query (multi-keyword phrase)

USE CASE:
User receives context but wants to explore more:
'The context you provided is helpful, but I want to see more examples of token refresh handling.'
→ They can run: cass search "token refresh" --days 60

INTEGRATION:
These queries appear at the end of markdown output under '### Dig Deeper' section
Also included in JSON output for programmatic access

ALTERNATIVE NAMES:
- 'Suggested Searches'
- 'Follow-up Queries'
- 'Explore More'","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.569423-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:04:56.113182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:03:40.649191-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b96v","title":"[CLI] mark command - Record bullet feedback","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:41.369847-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:17.521594-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:13.739773-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-b9dr","title":"Document HarmfulReason enum values in mark --help","description":"## Context

The `cm mark <id> --harmful --reason <text>` command accepts specific enum values for the reason parameter, but these aren't documented in the --help output.

## Current State

```
.option("--reason <text>", "Reason for feedback")
```

The valid values are defined in `src/types.ts` as `HarmfulReasonEnum`:
- `caused_bug` - The rule caused a bug to be introduced
- `wasted_time` - Following the rule wasted time without benefit
- `contradicted_requirements` - The rule contradicted actual requirements
- `wrong_context` - The rule was correct but wrong for this context
- `outdated` - The rule is no longer accurate/relevant
- `other` - Freeform reason (fallback)

If an agent passes an unrecognized value, it silently falls back to "other", losing semantic information.

## Proposed Change

Update `src/cm.ts` line 79:
```typescript
.option("--reason <text>", "Reason: caused_bug|wasted_time|contradicted_requirements|wrong_context|outdated|other")
```

## Why This Matters

- Agents can provide more precise feedback
- Aggregated feedback data is more useful when reasons are categorized
- No breaking changes - purely additive documentation

## Implementation

1. Edit src/cm.ts line 79 to include enum values in description
2. Optionally: add validation that warns if unrecognized value is passed (currently silent)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `cm mark --help` shows valid reason values
- [ ] Documentation matches actual enum in types.ts

## Effort Estimate

~5 minutes - trivial string change

## Part of Epic

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bb9","title":"[Cass] findUnprocessedSessions - Get sessions not yet reflected on","description":"Implement findUnprocessedSessions() to get sessions not yet reflected on.

## Signature
```typescript
interface ProcessedLog {
  processedSessions: Set<string>;
  lastProcessedAt?: string;
}

async function findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog: ProcessedLog,
  options: { days?: number; agents?: string[] },
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string[]>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Call cassTimeline(days) to get recent sessions
2. Extract all session paths from timeline groups
3. Filter out paths already in processedLog.processedSessions
4. Optionally filter by agent names
5. Return unprocessed session paths

## Error Handling
- Return empty array if timeline fails
- Handle missing timeline gracefully
- Log filtering progress at debug level

## Parameters
- processedLog: Tracks which sessions have been reflected on
- options.days: How far back to look (default: 7)
- options.agents: Filter by agent names (e.g., ["claude-code"])

## Algorithm
```typescript
const timeline = await cassTimeline(options.days || 7);
const allSessions = timeline.groups.flatMap(g => 
  g.sessions.map(s => s.path)
);

let unprocessed = allSessions.filter(path => 
  \!processedLog.processedSessions.has(path)
);

if (options.agents) {
  unprocessed = unprocessed.filter(path => {
    const session = timeline.groups
      .flatMap(g => g.sessions)
      .find(s => s.path === path);
    return options.agents.includes(session?.agent);
  });
}

return unprocessed;
```

## Usage
Core function for reflect command:
```typescript
const processedLog = loadProcessedLog();
const unprocessed = await findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog, 
  { days: 7, agents: ["claude-code"] }
);

for (const sessionPath of unprocessed) {
  await reflectOnSession(sessionPath);
  processedLog.processedSessions.add(sessionPath);
}

saveProcessedLog(processedLog);
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.582356-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:10:22.243784-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:09:23.214984-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","title":"[Utils] hashContent - Normalized content hashing for deduplication","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:27.269804-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:24.584913-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function hashContent(content: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Normalize content:
  - Convert to lowercase
  - Collapse multiple whitespace to single space
  - Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Create SHA-256 hash using crypto.createHash('sha256')
- Update hash with normalized content
- Return first 16 characters of hex digest

## Why This Matters
Critical for exact duplicate detection in curator. Normalization ensures "Test Content" and "test  content" produce same hash, catching duplicates that differ only in formatting. Used in deduplication pass before semantic similarity check.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty string - returns hash of empty string
- Only whitespace - normalized to empty, returns hash of empty
- Very long content - SHA-256 handles any size
- Unicode characters - ensure proper encoding (UTF-8)
- Newlines and tabs - collapsed to single space by normalization","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bcj","title":"[Generator] executeSearchPlan - Parallel cass searches","description":"Parallel execution of cass searches with deduplication.

SIGNATURE:
async function executeSearchPlan(queries: string[], config: Config): Promise<CassHit[]>

PURPOSE:
Execute all generated queries against cass in parallel, combine results, deduplicate, and return top hits sorted by relevance score.

FLOW:
1. Map queries to cass search promises with config.sessionLookbackDays
2. Execute all with Promise.all (parallel for speed)
3. Flatten results into single array
4. Deduplicate by source_path + line_number (same snippet from multiple queries)
5. Sort by relevance score descending
6. Return top N results (config.maxHistorySnippets or default 10)

CASS SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- limit: 5 per query
- days: config.sessionLookbackDays (default 90)
- agent: config.preferredAgent (optional filter)

DEDUPLICATION LOGIC:
Two hits are duplicates if:
  hit1.source_path === hit2.source_path && hit1.line_number === hit2.line_number
Keep the one with higher relevance score.

RESULT SHAPE:
Array<{
  session_path: string,
  agent: string,
  snippet: string,
  relevance: number,
  source_path: string,
  line_number: number
}>

ERROR HANDLING:
- If cass binary not found, throw error (caught by generateTaskContext)
- If individual query fails, log warning but continue with other queries
- Return empty array if all queries fail","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.025102-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:44.041487-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:33.686488-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","title":"[UX][onboarding] Restructure cm --help for progressive disclosure","description":"Problem
- The CLI has many commands, but the product story is “one command for agents” (`cm context`).
- Default help output doesn’t strongly guide new users and can feel overwhelming.

What to do
- Improve help information architecture:
  - Put agent workflow first (context + quickstart)
  - Group operator/maintenance commands separately (init/doctor/reflect/playbook)
  - Group advanced/rare commands last (serve/outcome/audit/validate)
  - Add 3-5 high-quality examples that match README

Constraints
- Keep commander usage intact; use built-in help hooks (`addHelpText`, `showHelpAfterError`, etc.).
- Use invoked binary name (depends on naming task).","design":"Implementation sketch
- Use commander’s `program.addHelpText("after", ...)` to print curated sections.
- Consider `program.showHelpAfterError(true)` and `program.showSuggestionAfterError(true)`.","acceptance_criteria":"- `cm --help` is noticeably clearer and highlights the “one command” path.
- Help examples are copy/paste correct.
- Errors show help suggestions without dumping stack traces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:20:47.859404-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T17:43:09.318407-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T17:43:09.318407-05:00","labels":["cli","onboarding","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-1ojv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:21:00.001777-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-2yzr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.153805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-une","title":"[Validator] validateDeltas - Batch validate multiple deltas","description":"Implement the validateDeltas function - Batch validation for multiple playbook deltas.

SIGNATURE:
```typescript
async function validateDeltas(
  deltas: PlaybookDelta[],
  config: Config
): Promise<Array<{ delta: PlaybookDelta; result: ValidationResult }>>
```

BATCH VALIDATION FLOW:
1. Filter deltas to only "add" type (others pass through)
2. For each delta requiring validation:
   - Run validateDelta(delta, config)
   - Collect result
3. Return array of delta + result pairs

RETURN TYPE:
```typescript
Array<{
  delta: PlaybookDelta;
  result: {
    valid: boolean;
    reason: string;
    evidence: string[];
    suggestedState: "draft" | "active";
  };
}>
```

PARALLELIZATION OPTIONS:
Sequential (default):
```typescript
const results = [];
for (const delta of deltas) {
  if (delta.type === "add") {
    const result = await validateDelta(delta, config);
    results.push({ delta, result });
  }
}
```

Parallel (optional optimization):
```typescript
const validationPromises = deltas
  .filter(d => d.type === "add")
  .map(delta => validateDelta(delta, config).then(result => ({ delta, result })));
const results = await Promise.all(validationPromises);
```

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Only validate deltas with type === "add"
- "remove" deltas pass through (valid: true)
- "edit" deltas could be validated (future enhancement)
- Non-add deltas get default result: { valid: true, reason: "No validation required" }

ERROR HANDLING:
- If one validation fails, continue with others
- Collect errors in result.reason
- Don't fail entire batch for single validation error

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Enables bulk rule processing from `cm learn`
- Supports batch imports from other sources
- Can parallelize for performance (if LLM API supports it)
- Provides consistent interface for single + batch operations

USE CASES:
- `cm learn` generating multiple rules from one session
- Importing rules from external playbooks
- Re-validating existing rules against new evidence
- Bulk operations on rule sets

DEPENDENCIES:
- validateDelta() - Single delta validation
- PlaybookDelta type
- Config type

LOCATION:
- src/validator/batch-validate.ts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.802201-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T14:28:28.546867-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T14:28:28.546867-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-unhc","title":"[CLI] top command - Most effective bullets","description":"Implements the 'cass-memory top' command to show the most effective bullets.

COMMAND SYNTAX:
```bash
cass-memory top [N] [options]

Arguments:
  N              Number of bullets to show (default: 10)

Options:
  --scope SCOPE  Filter by scope (global, workspace, all)
  --category CAT Filter by category
  --json         Output as JSON
```

PURPOSE:
Quick command to see which bullets are most effective based on current scores with decay applied. Useful for understanding what rules are working well.

OUTPUT FORMAT (Terminal):
```
TOP 10 MOST EFFECTIVE BULLETS
==============================

1. [Score: 12.4] Use React.memo for expensive renders
   Category: Performance | Scope: global | Maturity: proven
   Feedback: 8× helpful, 0× harmful
   Last used: 2 days ago

2. [Score: 11.8] Run migrations in transaction
   Category: Database | Scope: global | Maturity: proven
   Feedback: 7× helpful, 0× harmful
   Last used: 5 days ago

3. [Score: 10.2] Validate input at API boundary
   Category: Security | Scope: workspace | Maturity: established
   Feedback: 5× helpful, 1× harmful
   Last used: 1 day ago

...
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. Load playbook from storage
2. Calculate effective score for each bullet (with decay)
3. Filter by scope/category if specified
4. Sort by effective score descending
5. Take top N bullets (default 10)
6. Format and display

SCORE CALCULATION:
- Use effective score with time decay
- Formula: base_score * decay_factor
- Decay based on days since last feedback
- See score calculation utilities

DISPLAYED INFORMATION:
- Rank and score
- Bullet content (truncated to fit)
- Category and scope
- Maturity level
- Feedback counts (helpful vs harmful)
- Last used timestamp

FILTERS:
- --scope: global, workspace, or all
- --category: filter to specific category
- Can combine filters

JSON OUTPUT:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "count": 10,
  "bullets": [
    {
      "rank": 1,
      "id": "abc-123",
      "score": 12.4,
      "content": "Use React.memo for expensive renders",
      "category": "Performance",
      "scope": "global",
      "maturity": "proven",
      "feedback": { "helpful": 8, "harmful": 0 },
      "lastUsed": "2025-01-13T08:00:00Z"
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

EDGE CASES:
- Empty playbook → show message
- N > bullet count → show all
- All bullets negative scores → still show top N
- Ties in score → sort by ID or last used

DEPENDENCIES:
- Playbook loader
- Score calculation utilities
- Date formatting
- Terminal formatting (chalk, table)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Shows most effective bullets accurately
- Scores reflect current state with decay
- Filters work correctly
- Output is readable and informative
- Performance <500ms for 1000+ bullets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:56:52.057091-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T13:28:38.156941-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T13:28:38.156941-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-uom9","title":"Add solo-user workflow guidance to quickstart","description":"## Context

The quickstart command (`cm quickstart`) is designed to explain the system to AI agents. Currently it says:

> "Run `cm reflect` manually in a well-configured setup. An operator typically schedules it (cron/hook)."

This assumes a separation between "agent" and "operator" - but many users are SOLO users who are both. The current guidance leaves them confused about what to do.

## Problem

A solo user (common case: individual developer using Claude Code) reads the quickstart and sees:
1. "Use `cm context` before tasks" ✓ Clear
2. "Don't run reflect manually" - But how do rules get learned then?
3. "An operator schedules it" - But I AM the operator, what do I do?

## Proposed Addition

Add a new section to `src/commands/quickstart.ts`:

```markdown
## Solo Users (You're Both Agent and Operator)

If you're working alone without scheduled reflection:

**Option A: Manual Reflection (Recommended)**
\`\`\`bash
# After completing significant work:
cm reflect --days 1 --json

# Weekly maintenance:
cm reflect --days 7 --json
\`\`\`

**Option B: Agent-Native Onboarding**
\`\`\`bash
# Check what sessions need analysis:
cm onboard --status --json

# Sample sessions to review:
cm onboard --sample --json
\`\`\`

The system works without scheduled reflection - it just means you trigger learning manually.
```

## Why This Matters

- Most early adopters are solo users
- The current docs create confusion and friction
- Clear guidance reduces support burden
- Aligns with "agent-native" design philosophy

## Implementation

1. Add new section to `getQuickstartText()` in src/commands/quickstart.ts
2. Add corresponding fields to `getQuickstartJson()` for JSON output
3. Position after "What You Usually DON'T Need To Do" section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `cm quickstart` shows solo user guidance
- [ ] `cm quickstart --json` includes solo user info in JSON
- [ ] Guidance is actionable (specific commands to run)

## Effort Estimate

~30 minutes

## Part of Epic

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-usqd","title":"[E2E Tests] CLI mark command - feedback recording","description":"## E2E Test: CLI mark Command - Feedback Recording

### Test Scenario
Test `cm mark` records user feedback on bullets

### Test Steps

#### 1. Mark Positive Feedback
- Initialize project with playbook containing bullets
- Run `cm mark --helpful "bullet content snippet"`
- Verify bullet confidence increased
- Verify feedback event logged

#### 2. Mark Negative Feedback
- Run `cm mark --unhelpful "bullet content"`
- Verify bullet confidence decreased
- Verify feedback event logged

#### 3. Mark with Session Context
- Run `cm mark --helpful --session /path/to/session "bullet"`
- Verify session path recorded in event
- Verify helps future correlation

#### 4. Mark Non-existent Bullet
- Run `cm mark --helpful "non existent bullet"`
- Verify graceful error message
- Suggest similar bullets if available

#### 5. Fuzzy Matching
- Test partial content matching
- Test case-insensitive matching
- Verify correct bullet identified

### Verbose Logging
- Log: 'Recording feedback for bullet: <snippet>'
- Log: 'Searching playbook for matching bullet...'
- Log: 'Found matching bullet with confidence: X'
- Log: 'Applying feedback: +/- Y confidence'
- Log: 'New confidence: Z'
- Log: 'Feedback recorded successfully'

### Feedback Persistence
- Verify feedback survives restart

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","title":"[Test Infrastructure] Helpers, Fixtures & Logging Framework","description":"## Target: Shared test infrastructure for all test suites

### Helper Modules to Create:

1. **test/helpers/temp-env.ts** - Temporary environment management
   ```typescript
   export async function createIsolatedEnvironment(): Promise<TestEnv>
   export async function cleanupEnvironment(env: TestEnv): Promise<void>
   export async function withTempCassHome<T>(fn: (home: string) => Promise<T>): Promise<T>
   ```

2. **test/helpers/cli-runner.ts** - CLI execution helpers
   ```typescript
   export interface CLIResult {
     exitCode: number;
     stdout: string;
     stderr: string;
     duration: number;
   }
   export async function runCLI(args: string[], options?: RunOptions): Promise<CLIResult>
   ```

3. **test/helpers/logging.ts** - Detailed test logging
   ```typescript
   export function log(message: string, ...data: unknown[]): void
   export function logStep(step: string): void
   export function logTiming(operation: string, durationMs: number): void
   export function logFileState(path: string): void
   export function summarize(): TestSummary
   ```

4. **test/helpers/fixtures.ts** - Test data generators
   ```typescript
   export function generateBullet(overrides?: Partial<PlaybookBullet>): PlaybookBullet
   export function generatePlaybook(bulletCount: number): Playbook
   export function generateDiaryEntry(date?: Date): DiaryEntry
   export function generateFeedbackEvent(type: 'helpful' | 'harmful'): FeedbackEvent
   ```

5. **test/helpers/git.ts** - Git repository helpers
   ```typescript
   export async function createTempGitRepo(): Promise<string>
   export async function cloneRepo(source: string): Promise<string>
   export async function commitAll(repoPath: string, message: string): Promise<void>
   ```

6. **test/helpers/assertions.ts** - Custom assertions
   ```typescript
   export function expectValidPlaybook(playbook: unknown): asserts playbook is Playbook
   export function expectValidBullet(bullet: unknown): asserts bullet is PlaybookBullet
   export function expectFileContains(path: string, content: string): Promise<void>
   ```

### Logging Output Format:
```
[2024-01-15T10:30:45.123Z] [STEP] Starting: Initialize environment
[2024-01-15T10:30:45.456Z] [INFO] Created temp dir: /tmp/cass-test-abc123
[2024-01-15T10:30:45.789Z] [CLI] Running: cm init --json
[2024-01-15T10:30:46.012Z] [CLI] Exit: 0 (223ms)
[2024-01-15T10:30:46.012Z] [CLI] Stdout: {"success":true,...}
[2024-01-15T10:30:46.012Z] [FILE] ~/.cass-memory/config.json: 234 bytes
[2024-01-15T10:30:46.123Z] [PASS] Step completed successfully
```

### Configuration:
- LOG_LEVEL env var (debug, info, warn, error)
- LOG_FILE env var (optional file output)
- KEEP_TEMP env var (don't cleanup for debugging)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:37.249094-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:44:14.854078-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:42:52.573522-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-v319","title":"[ErrorHandling] handlePermissionError - File access issues","description":"Provide helpful error messages for permission-related failures.

Function signature:
function handlePermissionError(error: Error, path: string): never

Implementation:
- Detect EACCES and EPERM errors
- Get file/directory permissions using fs.stat
- Provide helpful message including:
  * Which file/directory failed
  * Current permissions (in readable format)
  * Suggested fix command (chmod/chown)
  * Example: 'chmod 644 /path/to/file' or 'sudo chown user /path'
- Throw enhanced error with actionable guidance

Common scenarios:
- Cannot read session files
- Cannot write to playbook
- Cannot create directories

Helps users quickly resolve permission issues.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:26.616724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T22:34:15.778554-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T22:33:56.769211-05:00"}

 The system can learn from sessions automatically **once** reflection is scheduled.
 
+## Solo Users (You're Both Agent AND Operator)
+
+If you're working alone without scheduled reflection:
+
+**Option A: Manual Reflection (Recommended)**
+\`\`\`bash
+# After completing significant work:
+${cli} reflect --days 1 --json
+
+# Weekly maintenance:
+${cli} reflect --days 7 --json
+\`\`\`
+
+**Option B: Agent-Native Onboarding**
+\`\`\`bash
+# Check what sessions need analysis:
+${cli} onboard status --json
+
+# Sample sessions to review:
+${cli} onboard sample --json
+
+# Read a session for rule extraction:
+${cli} onboard read <session-path> --json
+\`\`\`
+
+The system works without scheduled reflection - you just trigger learning manually.
+
